business.headline: Ujjawala LPG subsidy down to first 4 refills amid conflict-driven crude price surgeEarlier, PMUY beneficiaries received a Rs 300 subsidy on the first nine LPG refills annually. The government reduced this to four refills, citing average household consumption of about four cylinders per year.

India posts Q4 current account surplus as services exports surgeAccording to data released by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) on Monday, the country's current account recorded a surplus of USD 7.1 billion, or 0.7 per cent of GDP, in the fourth quarter of 2025-26.

Oil, growth, inflation: CEA explains India's key risks amid West Asia crisisSpeaking to India Today, CEA V Anantha Nageswaran said India's economy remains strong, but uncertainty around the ongoing conflict and energy prices continues to pose risks.

Air India may introduce 'no meals' tickets on domestic flights to cut faresAir India is considering a no-meals ticket category for domestic passengers. The plan could begin on select short routes and lower fares for flyers who skip onboard food.

Builder delayed possession? Here's what homebuyers can doBuilder delayed possession of your flat? Learn your rights under RERA, when you can seek compensation or a refund, whether you must continue paying EMI, and what legal and financial options homebuyers have when projects are delayed.

Oil prices cannot stay this high for long, says Oil Minister Hardeep Singh PuriWhile fuel prices remain elevated for now, Puri's comments suggest that policymakers expect the pressure to ease over time.

Want to invest in SpaceX's IPO? Here's what retail investors need to knowAccess to the SpaceX IPO is not limited to the United States. The company has indicated that qualified investors in several countries may be able to buy shares, subject to local regulations and eligibility requirements.

Sensex, Nifty hit 2-month lows as Iran-Israel tensions push crude oil near $100Markets closed at nearly two-month lows after fresh Israeli strikes on Iran and Lebanon pushed crude prices sharply higher. The sell-off deepened across sectors as investors weighed inflation risks, global tech weakness and the path of interest rates.

Economist's model got last 3 FIFA World Cup winners right. Here's its 2026 pickAs football fans debate whether Argentina can defend its title, whether England's golden generation can finally deliver, or whether France can add another star to its jersey, a German economist has come up with a prediction that is turning heads across the football world.

From AI trip planning to travel insurance: How are Indians redefining travel in 2026?Gone are the days when a holiday meant simply booking tickets and packing bags. Today's Indian traveller is more digital, more informed and more prepared, reshaping the travel landscape in the process.

India, US could finalise trade deal by July, says Commerce Minister Piyush GoyalThe Union Minister's remarks come at a time when India and the US are working to resolve outstanding issues in a proposed bilateral trade pact that is expected to improve market access and give Indian exporters an advantage over competitors in certain sectors.

AI stock sell-off hits Taiwan, South Korea after rally past IndiaAsian markets sank as investors dumped AI-linked chip stocks in Taiwan, South Korea and Japan. The sell-off has raised fresh questions over whether AI rally expectations had run ahead of earnings reality.

CIBIL score under 730? Getting a home or car loan could become tougher from 2027For many Indians, a home loan or car loan is often the biggest financial commitment they make. But from 2027, getting such loans could become more difficult if your CIBIL score is below 730, as banks prepare to follow stricter RBI guidelines on lending risk.

Adani Ports bags 10-year marine services deal for Argentina's first LNG exportsThe contract was awarded to Adani Harbour International FZCO, a step-down subsidiary of APSEZ, through a consortium formed with Argentina-based Meridian Group. The consortium won the contract after a global competitive bidding process conducted by Southern Energy S.A. (SESA).

Gold falls by Rs 1,800, silver crashes Rs 6,400: Why are precious metals falling?Gold and silver extended losses on Monday as stronger US jobs data altered rate expectations. The slide deepened as higher Treasury yields, Middle East tensions and inflation worries weighed on bullion.

Sensex falls 600 points: 3 reasons why stock market is falling todayMarkets opened sharply lower on Monday as rising Middle East tensions, higher crude prices and global weakness hit sentiment. The slide sharpened concerns over inflation, foreign outflows and pressure on technology stocks.

Stock market falls amid fresh Iran-Israel flare up, crude oil climbs above $96The selloff was broad-based, with almost all major stocks trading in the red. Investors turned cautious after fresh conflict in the Middle East pushed oil prices higher and raised concerns about inflation and global economic growth.
Stock markets likely to fall today: 3 reasons whyGIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,138 at 8:06 am IST, indicating that the Nifty 50 could open about 1% lower than its previous close of 23,366.70.

Crude oil rises over 3%. Is $100 a barrel back in sight?Brent crude rose 3.45% to $96.30 per barrel, while US benchmark WTI crude climbed 3.41% to $93.63 per barrel during trading as of 8:10 am. The sharp rebound erased most of Friday's losses, when oil prices had fallen on hopes that diplomatic efforts could ease tensions in the region.

Your flat is delayed. Your EMI isn't. What now?You're paying the EMI. The builder missed the deadline. With over 5 lakh homes stuck across India, delayed possession has become a financial nightmare for thousands of buyers. Here's what experts say you can do when your dream home doesn't arrive on time.

entertainment.headline: Kangana Ranaut admits to self-doubt in politics: Am I still too much of a heroine? BJP MP and actor Kangana Ranaut opened up about the self-doubt she experiences as a politician, revealing that she often questions whether she is meeting expectations in her new role.

Bandar irony: Bobby Deol's career-best role comes in a film no one's looking at In Bandar, Bobby Deol sheds familiar glamour to get into the skin of a role destined to highlight his career. The problem: Anurag Kashyap's grim film is yet to get people talking.

Katy Perry, Justin Trudeau break the internet with PDA-filled red carpet debut Katy Perry and Justin Trudeau have taken their romance to the next level with their first-ever red carpet appearance together at the Tribeca Festival. Here's what happened and why fans can't stop talking about it.

The Bear Season 5 trailer brings floods, financial woes and one last shot at success The Bear season 5 trailer teases mounting pressure, a flooded restaurant and a race against time. As the final season approaches, the team faces its toughest challenge yet.

Peddi isn't alone; KGF, Pushpa and the blockbuster habit of objectifying women The backlash against Peddi has put the spotlight on the portrayal of Janhvi Kapoor's character. More importantly, it has revived a larger conversation about how India's biggest blockbusters continue to sideline women in favour of larger-than-life heroes.

Dhanush calls Rafael Nadal his GOAT after watching Rafa documentary Dhanush's reaction to the Netflix documentary Rafa has caught fans' attention. The actor's emotional message reveals a new perspective on Rafael Nadal's career and legacy.

Owen Cooper makes Soccer Aid history as youngest-ever player in charity match Owen Cooper has etched his name into Soccer Aid history during the charity match's landmark anniversary year. Here's how the Adolescence star became part of a major milestone.

Varun Dhawan's proposal to Natasha did not go as planned. Actor spills details Varun Dhawan recalled how his proposal to Natasha Dalal in Alibaug did not go as planned and called it a bad proposal. The actor also said real-life proposals are often very different from what films show.

Nora Fatehi's FIFA World Cup track Siir Siir out: 'A full-circle moment' Nora Fatehi's latest track, Siir Siir, has been released as part of the official FIFA World Cup 2026 music project. The actor-performer also opened up about the song's cultural roots and the strong Indian representation behind it.

Rs 3,300 for a movie ticket? The Odyssey mania is already sweeping India Advance bookings for Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey opened across India with IMAX ticket prices touching Rs 3,300 in some cities. Despite the steep rates, several shows sold out within minutes, sparking debate and excitement online.

Padma Awards 2026: Mammootty, R Madhavan among film stars to be honoured on June 23 Mammootty, R Madhavan, Satish Shah and Rajendra Prasad are among the film personalities who will receive Padma honours from President Droupadi Murmu on June 23.

SlumDog 33 Temple Road teaser: Vijay Sethupathi turns a mysterious blind beggar The teaser of Puri Jagannadh's SlumDog: 33 Temple Road introduces Vijay Sethupathi as a visually impaired beggar. Billed as a pan-India release, the official release date of the upcoming film is yet to be announced by the makers.

Farah Khan recalls compliment from Michael Jackson: Still getting goosebumps Farah Khan recalled meeting Michael Jackson and the compliment he gave her during a recent vlog. She said the brief encounter still gives her goosebumps and remains one of her most cherished memories.

Idris Elba says James Bond shouldn't be "woke": Let's not try it Idris Elba said that the reports of him playing James Bond were never legitimate. He said the franchise should stay focused on escapism rather than chasing changing tastes.

Mom-to-be Deepika Padukone checks in on dream Bandra home with Ranveer Singh. Pics Deepika Padukone and Ranveer Singh checked the progress of their new Bandra home on Monday. Balcony pictures from the visit grabbed attention online for the actor's pregnancy glow and baby bump.

Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma welcomes rock band Guns N' Roses to Guwahati Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma welcomed the iconic rock Guns N' Roses to Guwahati for its upcoming concert. He said the show builds on recent momentum for Assam as a live entertainment destination and could boost tourism and the local economy.

Targeting Janhvi in Peddi row is unfair, she did what director said: Jagapathi Babu Actor Jagapathi Babu has defended Janhvi Kapoor amid online criticism over her role in Peddi, urging fans not to target her personally. He also said that an artist merely follows the director's orders, and it is unfair for her to be subjected to online trolling.

Karisma Kapoor on 90s Bollywood: No scripts, hardly any rehearsals Karisma Kapoor looked back on 1990s Hindi cinema and said many films were made without detailed scripts. She said the era stood out for its spontaneity, camaraderie and enduring superhits.

Anil Kapoor to host Indian adaptation of global game show The 1% Club Actor Anil Kapoor is set to return to television as the host of Star Plus' Indian adaptation of the global hit game show The 1% Club. The actor is currently shooting for the first promo, with an official announcement expected soon.

general news.headline: Bibi, be careful: Trump warns Netanyahu against striking Iran, escalating conflictUS President Donald Trump warned Netanyahu that Israel could end up isolated if it resumed large-scale strikes on Iran. The exchange exposed widening differences between Washington's push for diplomacy and Israel's threat of further force.

Khan Sir gets interim protection, arrest stayed in coaching centre firing caseThe relief came during a hearing on Khan's anticipatory bail plea before the District Judge's court. While granting interim protection, the court directed that police may continue questioning Faisal Khan as part of the investigation but cannot take coercive action against him, including arrest, until further orders.

Have some shame and spine: Mahua Moitra slams Yusuf Pathan as TMC MPs rebelMahua Moitra's remarks came hours after 20 Lok Sabha MPs, led by Chief Whip Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar, wrote to Speaker Om Birla declaring support for the BJP-led NDA, triggering a split in the TMC's parliamentary unit.

iPhone almost like a birth control device, fertility rates falling after 2007: ResearchGlobal birth rates have been declining, and new studies believe that your iPhone may be the culprit. Research hints that the iPhone may have played a role in falling birth rates ever since it was first launched in 2007.

Elon Musk flags India's declining fertility rate. What's driving the dip? How bad is it?Tesla boss Elon Musk's post on India's falling fertility rate has revived the debate over why fewer children are being born in India. Experts say the decline in the total fertility rate (TFR) is reflective of rising living costs, financial development, urban lifestyles and changing family aspirations and dynamics.

Swing from secular to Hindutva: How BJP won over the Bengali BhadralokThe BJP's 2026 breakthrough in West Bengal drew strong backing from the Bhadralok, long seen as sceptical of Hindutva politics. The shift reflected anti-incumbency against the Trinamool Congress, anger over jobs and corruption, and the impact of RG Kar protests and RSS mobilisation.

Hum Saath Saath Nahin Hain: Honeymoon turns family trip, bride seeks divorceA dream honeymoon turned into a nightmare when a newlywed bride discovered she would be sharing the trip with her husband's parents and siblings. The fallout has reached counselling sessions, with divorce now on the table.

Alcohol sales allowed in Muslim-majority Lakshadweep. Explained why rules changedFor nearly five decades, Lakshadweep was one of India's few territories where alcohol sales were largely prohibited. Around 97% of the population is Muslim. The Centre is now changing that, bringing a regulated licencing system as part of a broader push to boost tourism.

Peddi isn't alone; KGF, Pushpa and the blockbuster habit of objectifying womenThe backlash against Peddi has put the spotlight on the portrayal of Janhvi Kapoor's character. More importantly, it has revived a larger conversation about how India's biggest blockbusters continue to sideline women in favour of larger-than-life heroes.

Africa's best referee denied entry to USA, set to be ruled out of FIFA World CupAfrica's top referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan has reportedly been denied entry into the United States and sent back to Turkey ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026. The incident has intensified concerns over visa and travel issues affecting tournament personnel.

How 24 hours unravelled 60 days of uneasy peace between Iran and IsraelFor Iran, Beirut has long been the red line. Even after Iran and Israel exchanged strikes on the nights of June 7 and 8 before returning to a tenuous ceasefire, Tehran made clear that its patience was running thin. Iran's armed forces warned that any future Israeli attack on Lebanon would be met with even "harsher" retaliation.

POK on edge as 11 killed, 70 injured in violent clashes ahead of major protestAt least 11 people were killed and more than 70 injured during clashes between protesters and security forces in Rawalakot ahead of a region-wide shutdown planned for June 9, according to news agency Reuters.

Bandar irony: Bobby Deol's career-best role comes in a film no one's looking atIn Bandar, Bobby Deol sheds familiar glamour to get into the skin of a role destined to highlight his career. The problem: Anurag Kashyap's grim film is yet to get people talking.

What really happened at London nightclub Ben Stokes and England teammate visitedBen Stokes and Gus Atkinson are being investigated after a late-night altercation in London following England's win over New Zealand. The episode has delayed England's second-Test selection and reopened uneasy questions over leadership, discipline and control.

2026 El Nino could break records: Pacific Ocean could get warmer by 3.5°CThe Pacific Ocean is showing signs of significant warming, with the latest forecasts hinting at a climate pattern that could alter weather across the globe, and sending ripple effects far and wide.

Trump's $100,000 H-1B visa fee for skilled foreign workers thrown out by US judgeUS District Judge Leo Sorokin in Boston on Monday struck down the H-1B visa fee, which Trump announced in September as part of immigration crackdown.

Katy Perry, Justin Trudeau break the internet with PDA-filled red carpet debutKaty Perry and Justin Trudeau have taken their romance to the next level with their first-ever red carpet appearance together at the Tribeca Festival. Here's what happened and why fans can't stop talking about it.
UP man marries mother-in-law, video of court ceremony goes viralA man and his mother-in-law allegedly got married in a court ceremony in Uttar Pradesh's Kanpur.

Tea break, forgotten fryer: Chain of failures that turned B&B kitchen fire deadlyPolice say an unattended oil fryer and a 30-minute delay triggered the deadly Malviya Nagar hotel fire. The probe now centres on staff actions, safety compliance and whether the building itself worsened the blaze.

CM Vijay plays chess with Praggnanandhaa, impresses Norway Chess championTamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay felicitated R Praggnanandhaa in Chennai, awarded him Rs 50 lakh and played an impromptu game of chess. The meeting followed the grandmaster's historic Norway Chess title win, with Praggnanandhaa saying he was surprised by the Chief Minister's skill.

1 lakh customers bought this SUV: Offers 28kmpl efficiency, price starts at Rs 10.50 lakhMaruti Suzuki's latest SUV has crossed the 1 lakh sales milestone in just eight months. Priced from Rs 10.50 lakh and offering up to 28.65kmpl efficiency, the SUV has quickly become a strong seller.

Ujjawala LPG subsidy down to first 4 refills amid conflict-driven crude price surgeEarlier, PMUY beneficiaries received a Rs 300 subsidy on the first nine LPG refills annually. The government reduced this to four refills, citing average household consumption of about four cylinders per year.

Dressing up a massacre as military operation: India tears into Pakistan at UNIndia's Permanent Representative to the UN, Harish Parvathaneni, said that Pakistan's narrative was part of an effort to officially sponsor misinformation and disinformation dressed in religious terminology.

Israel plans a Shivaji statue. Untold story of Jews who fought in Maratha armyIsrael is preparing to erect a statue of Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj in the Jewish country, and bringing to spotlight an overlooked historical connection between Jews and India. The story stretches from the villages of Maharashtra to the armies and navies that helped shape India's history.

sports.headline: No rivals, beyond medals: How yogasana became more than a hunt for glory At the first World Yogasana Championship, athletes and coaches from 78 countries competed while often helping one another on and off the mat. The event suggested yogasana's strongest claim is not just medals, but a transnational culture of family, self-improvement and shared purpose.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is back: When and where to watch India A vs Sri Lanka A Vaibhav Sooryavanshi returns as India A open the Dambulla tri-series against Sri Lanka A on Tuesday. Tilak Varma will lead a young Indian side in the tri-series, which will also feature Afghanistan A side.

What really happened at London nightclub Ben Stokes and England teammate visited Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson are being investigated after a late-night altercation in London following England's win over New Zealand. The episode has delayed England's second-Test selection and reopened uneasy questions over leadership, discipline and control.

No Virat Kohli. Will Rohit Sharma play ODIs vs Afghanistan? India are awaiting medical clearance on Rohit Sharma for the Afghanistan ODI series. Hardik Pandya has been cleared, while Virat Kohli has been ruled out with a hamstring tear.

You're a gift to the sport: Andre Agassi hails Chwalinska's Roland Garros run Andre Agassi delivered an emotional message to Maja Chwalinska after her dream run to the Roland Garros final came to an end. The American legend hailed the Polish qualifier's historic achievement and called her "a gift to the sport."

Ben Stokes in trouble? England cricket probe 'nightclub incident' after Lord's Test England captain Ben Stokes and pacer Gus Atkinson are under investigation after an alleged breach of team protocols following the first Test against New Zealand. The incident has delayed squad decisions for the second Test and revived scrutiny of England's off-field culture.

Mandaviya backs club-led model to secure ISL future Union Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya has asked the AIFF and ISL clubs to form a joint task force to settle their financial dispute. The move is aimed at securing a stable business model before the 2026-27 season begins.

Africa's best referee denied entry to USA, set to be ruled out of FIFA World Cup Africa's top referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan has reportedly been denied entry into the United States and sent back to Turkey ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026. The incident has intensified concerns over visa and travel issues affecting tournament personnel.

They said you'd never win: Djokovic writes moving tribute to champion Zverev Following Alexander Zverev's historic French Open breakthrough, Novak Djokovic wrote a deeply personal tribute to his long-time friend, lauding the German's resilience in silencing critics who claimed he would never win a Grand Slam.

Shreyaka takes 4 as India spin to win against West Indies in T20 World Cup warm-up Women's T20 World Cup 2026: Bharti Fulmali's unbeaten fifty and Shreyanka Patil's four wickets gave India a 26-run warm-up win over West Indies in Cardiff. The outing offered useful game time to India's bench before matches against England and Pakistan.

Suryakumar Yadav answers T20I snub with Mumbai T20 League blitz Dropped from India's T20I squads for Ireland and England, Suryakumar Yadav struck an unbeaten 72 in the Mumbai T20 League at Wankhede. The innings revived his run of form and renewed focus on his place in India's plans.

India's delicate plan for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: Help his game, not ruin itIndia's batting coach Sitanshu Kotak said Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will be eased into the national set-up after his maiden T20I call-up. The team wants to protect the 15-year-old's natural game rather than overload him with advice.

Test team in transition? Sunil Gavaskar disagrees with coach Gautam Gambhir's takeAs India enter a crucial World Test Championship phase, batting legend Sunil Gavaskar has urged the young, evolving batting unit to step up and focus on long-form discipline rather than relying on the transition narrative.

Christian Eriksen in good spirits, set for discharge after mid-match heart scareChristian Eriksen is expected to be discharged from hospital soon after making a positive recovery from a mid-match collapse during Denmark's friendly against Ukraine. The Danish midfielder quickly regained consciousness, with doctors confirming he remains in stable condition.

Yogasana Super League: An IPL-themed competition to boost yoga's Olympic dreamYogasana Bharat says the Yogasana Super League is nearly ready for launch this year. The league sits at the centre of a broader push for Asian Games, Commonwealth Games and Olympic recognition.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's rise is dangerous for cricket: Greg ChappellFormer Australia cricketer Greg Chappell has warned that T20 cricket is approaching a tipping point, arguing that the format has become heavily skewed in favour of batters. Concerned about the growing imbalance between bat and ball, Chappell proposed three rule changes that he believes could restore balance to the game.

Gill reveals India's 350-run, 20-wicket Test formula after AFG routShubman Gill laid out India's 350-run, 20-wicket Test formula after the innings win over Afghanistan in Mullanpur. The result underlined India's bench strength and highlighted Manav Suthar's dream debut in a side in transition.

Manav Suthar stays grounded despite seven-wicket India Test debutManav Suthar starred on his India Test debut as his seven wickets sealed a crushing win over Afghanistan. Yet the left-arm spinner said the format demands patience, consistency and humility.

Innings and 300 runs: Suthar blows away Afghans inside 3 days on Test debutShubman Gill's India registered their biggest victory in Test cricket on Monday, June 8. Debutant Manav Suthar blew away Afghanistan with 7 wickets in Mullanpur.

Ignored for India, Shami makes his presence felt with hattrick in Bengal T20 LeagueMohammed Shami took a Bengal T20 League hat-trick for Siliguri Strikers against Rarh Tigers. The spell revived scrutiny of India's selection calls as the veteran remains outside ODI and Test plans.

Angry Mohammed Siraj riles up Rahmat Shah, triggers Afghanistan collapseIND vs AFG: Mohammed Siraj triggered Afghanistan's collapse on Day 3 of the Test match. He did not do it with his bat or ball, but with his mere words.

India captain snaps back at reporter over retirement question at Women's T20 WCHarmanpreet Kaur brushed aside retirement talk with a joke at a pre-tournament captains' media session. The exchange kept the focus on India's T20 World Cup build-up and the wider lift success has given women's cricket at home.

CM Vijay plays chess with Praggnanandhaa, impresses Norway Chess championTamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay felicitated R Praggnanandhaa in Chennai, awarded him Rs 50 lakh and played an impromptu game of chess. The meeting followed the grandmaster's historic Norway Chess title win, with Praggnanandhaa saying he was surprised by the Chief Minister's skill.

Manav Suthar becomes 2nd Indian this century to pick 5 wickets on Test debutIndia's debutant left-arm spinner ripped through Afghanistan with 6/33 and helped enforce the follow-on. The spell, backed by a handy 28, put him among India's best Test debutants.

Let Sooryavanshi be, don't expect the world of him: Sourav GangulyFormer India captain Sourav Ganguly has asked the world to temper his expectations ahead of Vaibhav Sooryavanshi potential India debut. Ganguly has said that playing for India will be different from playing in the IPL.

Flavio Cobolli follows Andreeva's example, thanks himself after French Open finalFrench Open 2026: Flavio Cobolli thanked himself and praised Alexander Zverev after a five-set French Open final defeat. The loss ended his title bid but confirmed a breakthrough fortnight that changed his standing in the game.

In Roland Garros, Zverev finds freedom in maiden Grand Slam winGermany's Alexander Zverev finally won his maiden Grand Slam. Playing against Italy's Flavio Cobolli, Zverev finally found the freedom that he had been looking for.

Why Praggnanandhaa's win in Norway Chess 2026 brings back belief of good times in Indian Chess?Norway Chess 2026: Praggnanandhaa created history when he won the Norway Chess 2026 event in grand style. Not only did he become the first Indian to win the prestigious tournament, he also defeated Magnus Carlsen in his sentinel twice and that deserves a special mention in the annals of chess.

Only Test, Day 3 Live: India win by innings and 100 runs in New ChandigarhIndia (IND) vs Afghanistan (AFG) Only Test Live Score and Updates, Day 3: It's all over in New Chandigarh. Afghanistan have lost by an innings and 300 runs. The Test match ends inside three days. Washington Sundar, Manav Suthar and Kuldeep Yadav run through the visitors' batting line-up.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and the summer of suspended disbeliefHandcrafted ByOur Editors In the summer of 2026, a boy from Bihar walked to the crease and gave Indian cricket back something it had quietly lost: the pure, uncomplicated joy of watching without needing to explain why.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
